title: Women’s Experiences of Intimate Partner Economic Abuse in the Eastern Region of Ghana
abstract: Despite its prevalence, intimate partner economic abuse has received less scholarly attention in sub-Saharan Africa. Using qualitative enquiry, this study describes the experiences of economic abuse among 18 women in the Eastern Region of Ghana. Economic abuse occurred in a variety of forms, including extortion, denial of income-earning activity, and attempts to deny women’s economic independence and financial self-sufficiency. Findings point to the pervasiveness of economic abuse among both high- and low-income women. While economic dependency among nonworking women was associated with sexual violence, working and independent women experienced physical and emotional violence on questioning intimate partner’s economic abuse.
authors_text: Sedziafa, Alice Pearl; Tenkorang, Eric Y.; Owusu, Adobea Y.; Sano, Yuji
